title: Time Keeps On Slippin

jokes.text: Those aren't swan boats. They're swans. / That explains these boat eggs.
jokes.setup: Fry mistakes real swans for swan-shaped boats and offers a romantic ride
jokes.punchline: The reveal that they're real swans, followed by Fry's absurd logic about boat eggs
jokes.explanation: Classic Futurama structure - sets up a romantic gesture, subverts with reality, then adds Fry's characteristic oblivious logic. Economy is perfect.

jokes.text: Sweet Clyde, laugh derisively at him.
jokes.setup: Professor challenges the Globetrotters
jokes.punchline: Bubblegum formally commands his teammate to laugh, treating derision as a basketball play
jokes.explanation: Perfect Globetrotter characterization - they treat everything like basketball moves, even insults. Very quotable structure.

jokes.text: I may be an old man... In fact, I'm fairly sure I am...
jokes.setup: Professor defending his capability despite age
jokes.punchline: His uncertainty about his own basic facts
jokes.explanation: Pure Professor character work - his senility manifests as uncertainty about obvious things. Well-integrated but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: They're only a foot high. / They're still young. Mere atomic superboys, really.
jokes.setup: Professor boasts about his mutant atomic supermen
jokes.punchline: Visual reveal they're tiny, followed by his matter-of-fact explanation
jokes.explanation: Great visual payoff to a setup, with Professor's characteristic scientific rationalization of failure. Strong structure and timing.

jokes.text: Those destroyed an entire civili... / Good news, everyone.
jokes.setup: Hermes starts to warn about Chronoton dangers
jokes.punchline: Professor cuts him off with his characteristic catchphrase, ignoring the warning
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and character integration. The Professor's obliviousness to danger is captured in the interruption structure.

jokes.text: Your face can take a lot of punishment. Good to know. / There's a lot about my face you don't know. You and it should get acquainted.
jokes.setup: Fry makes an unintentionally insulting comment about Leela's face
jokes.punchline: Leela's flirtatious response treating it as a come-on
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's confidence and ability to turn Fry's clumsiness into flirtation.

jokes.text: No, I mean, cool your jets... they're melting Bender's face.
jokes.setup: Fry thinks Leela is using an expression about calming down
jokes.punchline: She literally means his rocket jets are too hot and damaging Bender
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection taking a common expression literally in the sci-fi context. Clean structure.

jokes.text: The Trotters held a news conference to announce that I was a jive sucker.
jokes.setup: Professor explaining urgency of the basketball situation
jokes.punchline: The Globetrotters' formal, corporate approach to trash talk
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept - treating basketball trash talk as formal press announcements. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: in what should be a by-the-numbers contest with no surprises whatsoever.
jokes.setup: Sports announcer setting expectations for the game
jokes.punchline: Dramatic irony - audience knows chaos is coming in a show about time skips
jokes.explanation: Solid dramatic irony that works better on rewatch knowing the chaos to come. Standard sports commentary parody.

jokes.text: That one grabbed his behindus. / Quiet! Who dares laugh at the Jesters of Dunk?
jokes.setup: Announcer describes a basketball move with made-up anatomy
jokes.punchline: Bubblegum's offended reaction and self-referential title
jokes.explanation: 'Behindus' is a solid sci-fi anatomy gag, and 'Jesters of Dunk' is a great Globetrotter self-description.

jokes.text: We came to terrify and humiliate you, not tickle your funny bones.
jokes.setup: Globetrotters are known for entertaining basketball tricks
jokes.punchline: Their serious claim about intimidation vs. entertainment
jokes.explanation: Good character contradiction - Globetrotters trying to be serious despite their entertainment nature. 'Tickle your funny bones' is a nice phrase.

jokes.text: He's showing us what a man with a cannon in his chest can do.
jokes.setup: Mutant superman makes a basketball play
jokes.punchline: Deadpan sports commentary about having literal weaponry as body parts
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan treatment of the absurd. Sports announcers treating mutant anatomy as normal athletic ability.

jokes.text: No showboating, you atomic hotdog.
jokes.setup: Professor coaching his mutant team
jokes.punchline: Combining 'atomic' with the classic sports insult 'hotdog'
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective wordplay combining the sci-fi element with sports terminology. Quotable phrase.

jokes.text: Surprisingly dull so far.
jokes.setup: Announcing a game between space clowns and atomic mutants
jokes.punchline: Calling it 'dull' when it should be the most exciting thing ever
jokes.explanation: Good irony - the most fantastical matchup possible described as boring. Works as sports commentary parody.

jokes.text: Also, what game were the refs watching?
jokes.setup: Bubblegum explaining their strategy
jokes.punchline: Adding a completely normal sports complaint after the elaborate psychological explanation
jokes.explanation: Excellent bathos - follows the grandiose strategy with a mundane sports gripe. Nice structural contrast.

jokes.text: Did everything just jump around? Or did my brain just stroke off there?
jokes.setup: Everyone experiences the first time skip
jokes.punchline: Fry's characteristically self-aware but crude way of describing potential brain damage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry voice - considers both cosmic and medical explanations with equal crude directness. Very quotable phrasing.

jokes.text: We're lurching forward in time... like a needle skipping on a record.
jokes.setup: Professor explaining the time skip phenomenon
jokes.punchline: Using an outdated technology metaphor that younger characters wouldn't understand
jokes.explanation: Nice character detail - the ancient Professor uses record metaphors in the 31st century. Good sci-fi comedy writing.

jokes.text: Is it my outfit?
jokes.setup: Team trying to figure out cause of time skips
jokes.punchline: Amy's characteristic vanity making her assume it's about her appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy moment - making a cosmic crisis about her fashion choices. Great economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: Oops.
jokes.setup: Professor realizes he's caused a space-time catastrophe
jokes.punchline: His casual, understated response to potentially destroying reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement comedy. One word carries enormous comedic weight - the ultimate casual response to cosmic disaster. Peak Professor character work.

jokes.text: Now, on top of everything, we need a new fifth man.
jokes.setup: Atomic superman apparently vanishes due to time skip
jokes.punchline: Treating the loss of a team member as a minor basketball logistics issue
jokes.explanation: Great Globetrotter priority system - basketball logistics matter more than cosmic horror. Good understatement.

jokes.text: We're 35 points ahead with two minutes left. What could possibly...?
jokes.setup: Hermes expressing confidence about the game outcome
jokes.punchline: Classic 'tempting fate' setup that immediately leads to disaster
jokes.explanation: Standard 'tempting fate' trope but well-executed with the immediate time skip payoff. Predictable but functional.

jokes.text: Globetrotters win, 244-86. A dark day for humanity, folks.
jokes.setup: The score jumps impossibly high due to time skips
jokes.punchline: Announcer treating a basketball loss as genuinely apocalyptic for the species
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from sports loss to existential crisis. The massive score change sells the time skip concept.

jokes.text: Now all the planets are gonna crack wise about our mamas. / I'm glad my fat mama isn't alive.
jokes.setup: Hermes worries about intergalactic trash talk
jokes.punchline: Bender's relief that his mother won't be insulted because she's dead
jokes.explanation: Dark Bender humor - finding the silver lining in his mother's death. Good escalation from the setup about mama jokes.

jokes.text: Enough about your promiscuous mom...
jokes.setup: Following Bender's comment about his dead mother
jokes.punchline: Professor casually insulting the deceased mother while moving on to business
jokes.explanation: Professor's oblivious cruelty - insulting a robot's dead mother while trying to be businesslike. Good callback structure.

jokes.text: This chronological wang-dang-doodle could destroy the matrix of reality.
jokes.setup: Discussing the serious time disruption problem
jokes.punchline: Combining silly slang with legitimate scientific terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Globetrotter voice - mixing street slang with physics PhD knowledge. 'Chronological wang-dang-doodle' is brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: You're that Bubblegum Tate? / I sure ain't his grandma.
jokes.setup: Professor recognizes Bubblegum's academic credentials
jokes.punchline: Bubblegum's sassy confirmation using family relationship logic
jokes.explanation: Good Globetrotter attitude combined with logical certainty. The grandma reference adds character flavor.

jokes.text: At this rate, by Tuesday it'll be Thursday. By Wednesday it'll be August. And by Thursday, it will be the end of existence as we know it.
jokes.setup: Professor explaining how time skips will progress
jokes.punchline: Escalating from minor calendar confusion to universal apocalypse
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure - starts with mundane calendar confusion, builds to existential crisis. Perfect pacing and rhythm.

jokes.text: I have to ask you to clear out. The circus needs to set up for tomorrow's...
jokes.setup: Janitor interrupting the cosmic crisis discussion
jokes.punchline: Time skip cuts off his mundane announcement, showing the circus has already set up
jokes.explanation: Great visual storytelling through interruption. Shows time skips through mundane bureaucracy being overtaken by events.

jokes.text: teen singer Wendy might be the... Won three Grammys last night... Found dead in her bathtub.
jokes.setup: News reports skipping through time showing a celebrity's career
jokes.punchline: Rapid progression from rising star to success to death
jokes.explanation: Dark but effective demonstration of time skipping through compressed celebrity tragedy. Efficient storytelling device.

jokes.text: Interesting.
jokes.setup: Witnessing a celebrity's entire rise and fall in seconds
jokes.punchline: Professor's completely academic, detached response to human tragedy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor character moment - treating human tragedy as a scientific curiosity. One word carries great comedic weight.

jokes.text: Not if you grant me the same liberty.
jokes.setup: Bubblegum asks if he minds basketball dribbling while working
jokes.punchline: Professor requesting permission to dribble in the biological sense
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'dribble' with perfect Professor formality. The politeness makes the gross-out funnier.

jokes.text: Shut up, turkey.
jokes.setup: Fry asking to become a Globetrotter
jokes.punchline: Bubblegum's harsh but mild-sounding rejection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Globetrotter dismissal - insulting but using unexpectedly mild language. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: That sucker's shaking like fine booty.
jokes.setup: Describing scientific equipment fluctuations
jokes.punchline: Comparing technical readings to attractive anatomy with complete seriousness
jokes.explanation: Peak Globetrotter characterization - mixing scientific observation with street poetry. Incredibly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: I hope you find out what's wrong before we skip my birthd... / Happy birthday, Amy.
jokes.setup: Amy worries about missing her birthday due to time skips
jokes.punchline: Time immediately skips to her birthday, cutting off her complaint
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic timing - the thing she fears happens exactly as she mentions it. Great use of the time skip mechanic.

jokes.text: I hope we all have as much fun tomorrow at my birth... What?
jokes.setup: Amy trying to plan ahead after experiencing a time skip
jokes.punchline: Another immediate time skip proving planning is impossible
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the birthday gag - shows Amy can't learn from the pattern. Nice callback structure.

jokes.text: Because when we're together in here, baby, time will stand still.
jokes.setup: Fry shows Leela a 'time-proof shelter'
jokes.punchline: Cheesy romantic line that completely misses the point of temporal protection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry romantic clumsiness - using a genuine crisis as a pickup opportunity. Character-appropriate bad poetry.

jokes.text: does the worst W.C. Fields imitation I've ever seen. Zoidberg?
jokes.setup: Leela apparently watching Zoidberg perform during a time skip
jokes.punchline: Her critical review of his impression and confusion about missing time
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Leela being critical even during unconscious time skips. Shows personality persists.

jokes.text: I love his boyish charm, but I hate his childishness.
jokes.setup: Leela explaining her feelings about Fry
jokes.punchline: Drawing a distinction between positive and negative aspects of immaturity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character writing - captures the paradox of finding someone both attractive and frustrating. Very quotable insight.

jokes.text: So he's not perfect. You don't wanna end up old and lonely like Zoidberg!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg giving Leela relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Using himself as a cautionary example of loneliness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg self-awareness - he knows he's pathetic but tries to help anyway. Good character depth.

jokes.text: What's that funky jazz?
jokes.setup: Fry asked to smell something through a device
jokes.punchline: Describing a cosmic odor as 'funky jazz' with complete sincerity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry voice - mixing 20th century slang with cosmic concepts. The musical metaphor is unexpectedly poetic.

jokes.text: Let Bubblegum tell it. / Pretend these balls are time particles.
jokes.setup: Complex scientific explanation needed
jokes.punchline: Bubblegum uses basketball as a physics demonstration tool
jokes.explanation: Great character integration - Globetrotter physicist explains cosmic forces through basketball metaphors. Perfect fusion of his identities.

jokes.text: You are so smart.
jokes.setup: Bubblegum's basketball physics explanation
jokes.punchline: Amy's ditzy, genuine admiration for the demonstration
jokes.explanation: Simple Amy character moment - her superficial attraction to intelligence. Economical but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Moving stars requires a bad-ass gravity pump.
jokes.setup: Discussing the technical requirements for their plan
jokes.punchline: Combining street slang with astrophysics terminology
jokes.explanation: Another perfect Bubblegum moment - 'bad-ass gravity pump' combines his street and academic personas. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: We can't spend all of Earth's money every day.
jokes.setup: Nixon providing funding for the gravity pump
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that they regularly spend the planet's entire treasury
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist economics - implies they normally spend all Earth's money daily. Perfect deadpan delivery of impossible concept.

jokes.text: A jim-jam this complex might take months or even...
jokes.setup: Bubblegum explaining how long construction will take
jokes.punchline: Time skip cuts him off, revealing it's instantly completed
jokes.explanation: Good use of time skip mechanics for comedy. 'Jim-jam' is a nice Globetrotter slang term for complex equipment.

jokes.text: Off you go, apparently.
jokes.setup: Professor was about to explain the attachment process
jokes.punchline: Resigned acceptance that time skips have made his explanation pointless
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor reaction to being made irrelevant by circumstances. The resignation is perfectly characterized.

jokes.text: Hello, lawsuit.
jokes.setup: Fry shows off his homemade Globetrotter uniform
jokes.punchline: Bubblegum immediately recognizing trademark infringement
jokes.explanation: Good real-world awareness joke. Addressing costume copyright with business-like directness. Very quotable phrase.

jokes.text: Moderately priced, non-vintage champagne? How did you know?
jokes.setup: Fry gives Leela champagne as a gift
jokes.punchline: Leela's precise description emphasizes how unimpressive the gift is
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Leela's practical nature showing through even when being appreciative. The specificity is funny.

jokes.text: You're a man. I'm a woman. We're too different.
jokes.setup: Fry asking Leela out romantically
jokes.punchline: Reducing all relationship incompatibility to basic biology
jokes.explanation: Funny oversimplification of relationship complexity. The deadpan delivery of obvious facts makes it work.

jokes.text: I got her champagne. What does a guy have to do?
jokes.setup: Fry's romantic gesture fails to work
jokes.punchline: His genuine confusion about romance despite using a classic romantic cliché
jokes.explanation: Good Fry character work - his romantic inexperience despite following conventional wisdom. Relatable frustration.

jokes.text: Eighty-six the chump stuff. It's time to win Leela's heart with a showy, three-point romantic gesture.
jokes.setup: Fry lamenting his romantic failures
jokes.punchline: Bubblegum mixing basketball terminology with relationship advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Globetrotter advice - treating romance like basketball strategy. 'Three-point romantic gesture' is brilliantly specific.

jokes.text: there is absolutely, positively no way that you and I will ever... / man and wife. You may kiss the bride.
jokes.setup: Leela emphatically rejecting any possibility of romance with Fry
jokes.punchline: Time skip cuts to their wedding ceremony, completing her sentence ironically
jokes.explanation: Masterful misdirection and irony. The time skip creates perfect dramatic irony by completing Leela's sentence in the opposite way. Peak comedy writing.

jokes.text: And hypnosis is for losers with big eyebrows.
jokes.setup: Fry denying he used drugs or hypnosis to win Leela
jokes.punchline: His oddly specific insult about hypnotists' appearance
jokes.explanation: Typical Fry non-sequitur - the specific physical detail about eyebrows is randomly judgmental in a funny way.

jokes.text: The divorce is final.
jokes.setup: Fry expressing happiness about their marriage
jokes.punchline: Time skip immediately jumps to divorce proceedings
jokes.explanation: Brutal escalation of the marriage gag. The timing devastates Fry's romantic moment with legal finality.

jokes.text: I'll take the NFC helmets, and you can have the AFC.
jokes.setup: Dividing up possessions in divorce
jokes.punchline: Their 'china' collection is football helmets, showing their relationship's odd dynamics
jokes.explanation: Great character detail - shows they collected sports memorabilia as fancy dinnerware. Reveals their unconventional relationship.

jokes.text: but you still have Zoidberg. You all still have Zoidberg.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg trying to console heartbroken Fry
jokes.punchline: His pathetic attempt to position himself as a consolation prize
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg pathos - his desperate attempt to be valued, even as a replacement for lost love. Cringey but endearing.

jokes.text: I thought you knew that algebra was all razzmatazz. A Globetrotter always saves the good algebra for the final minutes.
jokes.setup: Professor complaining about the mathematical errors
jokes.punchline: Bubblegum treating mathematics like basketball showmanship with timing strategies
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Globetrotter characterization - treating mathematical accuracy like basketball strategy. 'Razzmatazz algebra' is peak comedy writing.

jokes.text: Stupid senior citizens! Why should we pay for their benefits? I deserve free money!
jokes.setup: Time skip shows Cubert has aged rapidly
jokes.punchline: He's become the thing he's complaining about while still maintaining his selfish attitude
jokes.explanation: Great irony - Cubert becomes a senior citizen while still hating senior citizens. Perfect character consistency through time.

jokes.text: An explosion big enough to destroy that mama would take out half the universe.
jokes.setup: Discussing how to destroy the nebula
jokes.punchline: Using 'mama' as slang for the nebula while discussing universal destruction
jokes.explanation: Good Globetrotter slang usage - 'mama' for large things fits their speech patterns while discussing cosmic destruction.

jokes.text: You got to Globetrotter that explosion up a little. Make it an implosion.
jokes.setup: Team needs a solution to the explosion problem
jokes.punchline: Using 'Globetrotter' as a verb meaning to improve with style
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay - 'Globetrotter' as a verb meaning to add style and effectiveness. Perfect character voice and highly quotable.

jokes.text: I suppose I could part with one and still be feared.
jokes.setup: Asked to provide a doomsday device
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that he has multiple world-ending weapons
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor character work - treating doomsday devices like collectibles and worrying about his reputation.

jokes.text: Too late, hot plate.
jokes.setup: Fry arriving just after everyone became honorary Globetrotters
jokes.punchline: Bubblegum's rhyming dismissal using temperature metaphor
jokes.explanation: Classic Globetrotter rhyming rejection. 'Hot plate' is unexpectedly creative rhyme slang. Very quotable.

jokes.text: But ask yourself, are you funky enough to be a Globetrotter? / Are you? / I mean, with time my funk level... / Are you? / No. / Deal with it.
jokes.setup: Fry being tested for Globetrotter worthiness
jokes.punchline: His confidence crumbling under repeated questioning about 'funkiness'
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure - Fry's confidence systematically destroyed through repetition. The 'funk' measurement is absurd but serious.

jokes.text: My life, and, by extension, everyone else's, is meaningless.
jokes.setup: Fry devastated by rejection from Globetrotters
jokes.punchline: Extending his personal despair to universal meaninglessness
jokes.explanation: Good Fry self-centeredness - making his personal failure into a cosmic crisis. The logical extension is funny.

jokes.text: Sorry, buddy. I too know what it's like to have a dream I'll never achieve.
jokes.setup: Bubblegum consoling depressed Fry
jokes.punchline: Elite Globetrotter physicist claiming to have unachievable dreams
jokes.explanation: Good irony - the most accomplished character claiming kinship with failure. Shows unexpected depth and empathy.

jokes.text: That's how I did it. I moved the stars to write her a love note.
jokes.setup: Fry finally remembering his grand romantic gesture
jokes.punchline: The most impossibly romantic act imaginable - rearranging stars as a love letter
jokes.explanation: Peak romantic comedy writing - the ultimate grand gesture that justifies the entire time skip plot. Emotionally perfect and cosmically scaled.

jokes.text: Did you see it? / The explosion? / No, not the explosion. / Then what? / Nothing.
jokes.setup: Fry desperate to know if Leela saw his star message
jokes.punchline: The cosmic love letter was destroyed in the explosion, leaving only 'nothing'
jokes.explanation: Devastating emotional payoff - the most romantic gesture ever is reduced to literal 'nothing.' Perfect tragic comedy structure.

tmdb_overview: While creating a team of mutants to play the Harlem Globetrotters, the Professor accidentally causes a disruption in time that threatens the existence of the universe. Meanwhile, Fry tries to win an unreceptive Leela's heart.
